meta.description: Each question contains exactly one blank represented by '__________'. The answer field contains the single correct technical term or phrase that completes the statement.
meta.grading_note: Matching should be case-insensitive. For answers with parenthetical abbreviations, accept either the abbreviation or the full form.

questions.question: The aviation communication-navigation-surveillance triad is abbreviated __________.
The merger of communication, navigation, and surveillance services onto shared spectrum is called spectrum __________.
Radios that implement waveforms in software are called __________ radios.
The limited availability of suitable aviation radio frequencies is known as spectrum __________.
A common integrated platform improves cross-service __________ and reduces regional incompatibility.
The converged CNS architecture is designed to improve system __________ after failures or interference.
An integrated platform that can be upgraded as standards evolve provides __________.
The UN civil aviation standards body is the International Civil Aviation __________.
The treaty-level spectrum authority that allocates aviation bands is the International Telecommunication __________.
The U.S. civil aviation regulator is the __________.
The European aviation regulator is __________.
The North American standards body that develops MOPS is __________.
The European counterpart that often works with RTCA is __________.
The air traffic controllers' federation is __________.
The pilots' association commonly abbreviated ALPA is __________.
The IP-based aviation information-sharing framework is called __________.
The global modernization initiative abbreviated ASBU is the Aviation System Block __________.
The component that optimizes traffic flow and demand-capacity balance is Air Traffic Flow __________.
The airspace function that allocates airspace resources among user groups is Airspace __________.
The communication quality specification for an ATM application is Required Communication __________.
The aviation mobile route service is abbreviated __________.
The civil voice allocation from 118 to 137 MHz is the __________.
The over-the-horizon aviation allocation below 30 MHz is the __________.
The congested 960 to 1215 MHz aviation allocation is the __________.
The satellite band spanning 26 to 40 GHz is the __________.
One nautical mile equals __________ meters.
One foot equals __________ meters.
Power expressed relative to one milliwatt uses the unit __________.
Power expressed relative to one watt uses the unit __________.
The logarithmic unit used for ratios in radio engineering is the __________.
The ideal lossless reference antenna is the __________ radiator.
The geometric spreading loss in free space is called __________ path loss.
Free-space path loss increases by about __________ dB each time distance doubles.
Free-space path loss increases by 20 dB per __________ of frequency.
Power flux density is usually abbreviated __________.
The impedance of free space is about __________ ohms.
The approximate Earth radius used in radio horizon calculations is __________ km.
The propagation model that depends on direct visibility is line-of-__________.
The curved propagation limit in line-of-sight planning is the radio __________.
The polarization produced by a rotating electric field is __________ polarization.
The bending of radio waves as they pass through different media is __________.
The mechanism that sends signals back from surfaces is __________.
The low-frequency mode that follows the Earth's curvature is __________ propagation.
Long-range propagation through the ionosphere is called __________ propagation.
The atmospheric layer that enables sky-wave communication is the __________.
The apparent frequency shift caused by relative motion is the __________ effect.
The quantity that compares signal power to noise power is signal-to-noise __________.
The theorem that links capacity to bandwidth and SNR is the Shannon __________ theorem.
The service metric that measures end-to-end transfer delay is __________.
The variation in packet delay is called __________.
Signal loss caused by rainfall is called rain __________.
Absorption that peaks around 6 GHz and 13 GHz is oxygen __________.
Rain fading is especially severe in the __________.
The path that supports communication below 30 MHz via the ionosphere is sky __________.
The component of a wave that changes apparent frequency under motion is the Doppler __________.
Compensation for motion-induced frequency shift is Doppler __________.
The standard aviation analog voice modulation is DSB-__________.
The more bandwidth-efficient alternative to full-carrier AM is SSB-__________.
The modulation family that changes signal phase in discrete steps is phase shift __________.
The modulation family that changes signal frequency in discrete states is frequency shift __________.
The Gaussian-filtered version of FSK used in some aeronautical links is __________.
The measure of channel width available to carry information is __________.
The ratio of signal power to noise power is signal-to-noise __________.
The theorem that bounds channel capacity is the Shannon __________ theorem.
The service metric that measures end-to-end transfer delay is __________.
The service metric that measures delay variation is __________.
The service metric that measures successful delivery over time is __________.
The service metric that measures delivered bits per second is __________.
The service metric that measures time a link is usable is __________.
The classical queuing model used for blocking probability is Erlang __________.
The extra signal level headroom used to survive fading is __________ margin.
The process of shaping audio before transmission to improve intelligibility is __________.
The filter added before modulation to reduce high-frequency emphasis is pre-__________.
The complementary filter applied on reception is de-__________.
The receiver function that mutes noise when no carrier is present is __________.
The civil aeronautical voice band from 118 to 137 MHz is the __________.
The dominant propagation model for airborne VHF links is __________-of-sight.
The European VHF channel spacing that increases capacity is 8.33 __________.
The legacy North American VHF channel spacing is 25 __________.
The earlier wide VHF channel split used 50 __________.
The earlier wide VHF channel split used 100 __________.
The oldest channelization step mentioned in the lecture was 200 __________.
Legacy aeronautical VHF voice uses DSB-__________.
The audio preprocessing that compresses then expands dynamic range is __________.
The receiver stage that provides selectivity around 10.7 MHz is the intermediate __________ stage.
The first active receiver stage that sets noise figure is the low-__________ amplifier.
The circuit that holds output audio level steady under fading is automatic gain __________.
The circuit that silences the receiver when no carrier is present is __________.
The minimum quality metric often cited for VHF receiver sensitivity is 6 dB __________.
The technique that lets one controller feed multiple receivers is a __________ network.
The method that broadcasts one signal from multiple transmitters on slightly offset carriers is __________.
The term for pairing frequencies so the strongest receive path is selected is frequency __________.
The measure of peak aircraft count used in sector planning is __________.
The three planning parameters for VHF dimensioning are coverage, capacity, and __________.
The VHF receiver ability to decode older wider channels is called backward __________.
The part of the speech spectrum concentrated below 4 kHz is the voice __________.
The controller site that normally works at a fixed frequency is the ground __________.
The cockpit frequency selector on a VHF radio is the thumbwheel __________.
The planned channel-splitting limit reached for legacy DSB-AM is 8.33 __________.
The European body managing the transition to 8.33 kHz is the ICAO Europe Frequency Management __________.
The first proprietary aeronautical datalink standard was __________.
ACARS is also known as VDL __________.
The ICAO mode that was standardized but never implemented was VDL __________.
The global ATN data link using 8DPSK and CSMA is VDL __________.
The TDMA voice-and-data mode that remained largely unimplemented is VDL __________.
The data-only surveillance-oriented mode is VDL __________.
The modulation used by VDL2 is __________.
The access protocol used by VDL2 is __________.
The multiple-access method for VDL3 is __________.
The access method for VDL4 is __________.
The nominal raw throughput of VDL2 is __________.
The VDL3 raw symbol rate is __________.
The channel width used by VDL2 is __________.
The VDL4 air interface rate is __________.
The VDL4 modulation is __________.
The VDL4 frame structure is a 60-second __________.
The VDL4 network contains __________ slots per super-frame.
The VDL4 common signaling resource is the global signalling __________.
The data link service that replaces routine voice clearances is __________.
The digital flight information service is abbreviated __________.
The terminal weather information service is abbreviated __________.
The airline operational data function is __________.
The FEC code used in VDL3 management bursts is __________.
The FEC code used in VDL3 data bursts is __________.
The VDL2 reliability feature that spreads frames across time is __________.
The beyond-line-of-sight voice band used for oceanic flying is the __________ band.
The HF voice modulation standard is __________.
The selective calling system used on HF is __________.
The ICAO-defined oceanic HF coverage regions are __________.
The weather broadcast service for long-range flights is __________.
The propagation mode enabling HF over the horizon is ionospheric __________.
The ground installation that services satellite calls is a ground __________.
The satellite orbit that appears fixed relative to Earth is __________.
The civil satellite safety service is __________.
The non-safety passenger satellite service is __________.
The primary mobile satellite band for aviation is the __________.
The reliability-oriented satellite band is the __________.
The capacity-oriented satellite band is the __________.
The high-speed satellite band is the __________.
Signal loss in Ku and Ka bands during precipitation is called rain __________.
The satellite ground earth station is abbreviated __________.
The packet signaling channel for link setup is the __________ channel.
The random access signaling channel is the __________ channel.
The time-slotted data channel is the __________ channel.
The circuit-mode satellite channel is the __________ channel.
The random-access protocol used on the R channel is slotted __________.
The low-rate satellite voice mode specified for aviation is __________.
The higher-rate satellite modulation used above 2400 bps is __________.
The GEO satellite path delay for AMS(R)S calls is typically 240 to 280 __________.
The call control feature that can interrupt lower-priority satellite traffic is __________.
The 30 to 88 MHz tactical military band is __________.
The 225 to 400 MHz anti-jam tactical voice system is __________.
The 420 to 450 MHz secure data network is __________.
The 960 to 1215 MHz UHF tactical data system is __________.
The smaller software-defined successor to JTIDS is __________.
The tactical data link used by JTIDS and MIDS is __________.
The Link 16 access method that divides transmission by time is __________.
The anti-jam technique that rapidly changes carrier frequencies is frequency __________.
The multiple-access method that assigns unique codes is __________.
The network property that avoids a single point of failure is __________ architecture.
The Link 16 function that estimates position relative to other nodes is __________ navigation.
The encrypted spoken option carried by Link 16 is secure __________.
The duration of one Link 16 slot is 7.8125 __________.
A Link 16 frame contains 1536 time __________.
Link 16 hops across 51 distinct __________.
The latency-critical access mode used for push-to-talk is demand assigned __________.
The reserved per-mobile access mode is dedicated __________.
The statistical sharing mode for slot contention is random __________.
The randomized offset before a message in Link 16 is __________.
The network master clock in the synchronization stack is the Network Time __________.
The default low-rate, high-immunity Link 16 packing format is standard double __________.
The higher-throughput Link 16 format with no duplication is packed 2 single __________.
The highest-throughput Link 16 format is packed 4 single __________.
The throughput of standard double pulse is 26.88 __________.
The throughput of packed 2 single pulse is 53.76 __________.
The link that connects mobile base stations to core networks is terrestrial __________.
The backbone infrastructure of global aeronautical networks is the core ground __________.
The aviation backbone that supports future air navigation connectivity is __________.
The satellite terminal system used where fiber is impractical is __________.
The short-distance wired bearer used between a base station and core equipment is copper __________.
The legacy trunking method that grouped analogue circuits into frequency division multiplex __________ is rare today.
The encoding mechanism that represents data for transmission and storage is pulse code __________.
The international PCM trunk standard with 32 time slots is __________.
The U.S. equivalent of E1 is __________.
The networking protocol suite underlying modern aviation data transport is __________.
The TCP/IP layer that hosts HTTP and FTP is the __________ layer.
The TCP/IP layer that handles reliable delivery is the __________ layer.
The TCP/IP layer that routes packets is the __________ layer.
The TCP/IP layer that maps to Wi-Fi and Ethernet access is the __________ layer.
The directional radio bearer used for airport and ATC links is microwave __________.
The extra power allowance that protects microwave links from fading is __________ margin.
The engineering analysis that checks received signal power is link __________.
The mobility protocol that keeps an aircraft's IP session stable across networks is __________.
The extension of MIPv6 that supports an entire aircraft network is __________.
The flight-test communications systems using airborne-to-ground data links are aeronautical telemetry __________.
The telemetry operating band around 2.3 to 2.4 GHz is the __________.
The telemetry waveform proposed for better spectrum efficiency is coded orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing, abbreviated __________.
The future UAV telecommand uplink spectrum is planned in the __________ band.
The signal impairment caused by reflections in urban canyons is GNSS __________.
The airborne network mobility problem addressed by MIPv6 and NEMO is session __________.
